game: The Legend of Zelda: A Link to the Past

v1.0.0.description:
Initial release of the new APWorld for The Legend of Zelda: A Link to the Past, based on the Overworld Randomizer.

Features:
- Goal options: Crystals, Ganon, All Dungeons, Pedestal, Triforce Hunt, Ganon Hunt, Trinity, Completionist
- Standard and Inverted game modes
- Set the number of crystals required for GT/Ganon, and number of Triforce pieces
- Any variety of keysanity, including key drop shuffle
- Enemy and boss shuffle, including logical enemy shuffle
- Crossed entrance shuffle, with an option for Zelgawoods
- Options for Pseudoboots and Mirror Scroll
- FastROM
- AP items are visually distinct: progressive items appear as green clocks, useful items as blue clocks, and filler/trap items as red clocks
- Can be generated without a ROM file

Known Bugs:
- Some items don't send until you leave the room they're picked up in
- Triforce Pieces and Small Keys can't be in the starting inventory
- The spoiler log shows a lot of events that should be hidden
v1.0.0.title: v1.0.0

v1.1.0.description:
New options:

* Custom sprites for Link
    * This takes the exact name of any sprite listed at https://alttpr.com/en/sprite_preview, and sets it as Link's sprite
    * If the person generating the seed does not have ALttP sprites downloaded, the selected sprite will be downloaded and saved to their Archipelago installation
    * If any errors occur while downloading or reading the sprite file, generation will continue with the vanilla Link sprite
    * This feature will likely have more changes and improvements in the future, so please let me know if you have any feedback
* Heart beeping rate
* Heart color
* Menu speed
* Disable music
* MSU resume

Bug fixes:
* Fixed a bug where if multiple ALttP players were in the same multiworld, only one of them could connect
* Fixed a bug where player names weren't displayed correctly when receiving items
v1.1.0.title: v1.1.0

v1.2.0.description:
New Options:
* Shopsanity
  * Random items now appear in all shop slots, including the potion shop and capacity upgrade fairy
  * 32 items are added to the item pool
  * 5 of the shop items will always be red/green/blue potions and arrow/bomb capacity upgrades, which can be bought repeatedly
* Prize Shuffle: adds Pendants and Crystals to the item pool.
   * Each dungeon is associated with a random Pendant or Crystal. After beating a dungeon, its matching prize's location will appear on the map screen, if it's in your world.
   * Saha and the Bomb Shop owner will tell you the location of the Green Pendant and Red Crystals, respectively.
* Random Flute spots
* Pre-activated Flute

Other Improvements:
* When reading a tablet or pedestal that has an AP item, it now says the item's name and owner
* Reaching Ganon's final phase with a bow will now tell you the location of silver arrows located in another player's world
* AP item names are now displayed in the credits where appropriate, if their name fits on screen
* Fixed a bug where minimal accessibility may require killing Ganon without silvers
* Removed some unneeded files from the APWorld
v1.2.0.title: v1.2.0

v1.2.1.description: Fixed a bug where boss prizes weren't getting sent in prize shuffle.
v1.2.1.title: v1.2.1

v1.2.2.description:
## Improvements
* (Beta) Universal Tracker support
  * Does not work with logical enemizer
  * Very barebones for now; no maps, yamlless tracking, /explain, or deferred entrances
* Generation is now deterministic per-seed
* Invalid YAML options now throw an error on generation
* Logical enemizer + standard start can no longer get BK'd in the Escape sequence

## Bug Fixes
* Shopkeepers have embraced capitalism and stopped giving their wares away for free
* Fixed a bug where locations locked behind blue crystal blocks in Swamp/Ice/Mire were wrongly considered in logic
* Fixed a bug where one-way doors were logically considered two-way, which could incorrectly treat Hyrule Castle, left side Mire, or Turtle Rock big chest room as accessible
* Fixed a bug in crossed entrances where if a cave/dungeon connected the light and dark worlds, any locations inside it logically required Moon Pearl
* Fixed a bug in crossed entrances where Hyrule Castle didn't logically require Moon Pearl if every entrance was in the dark world
v1.2.2.title: v1.2.2

v1.2.3.description: Fixed a bug where the client would crash when receiving an item from a player with an ID above 255.
v1.2.3.title: v1.2.3

v1.3.0.description:
# v1.3.0

## Doors!!!
* New option door_shuffle randomizes the layout of every dungeon
  * Rooms connect to each other in different ways, and any door between rooms might require a key or bombs
  * Each dungeon has its vanilla entrance room (e.g. Swamp Palace requires Flippers to enter), and all boss rooms are in their usual dungeon
  * Basic door shuffle randomizes each dungeon's layout, but each room stays within its own dungeon
  * Partitioned door shuffle can mix together rooms from different dungeons. Light World dungeons are shuffled together, early Dark World dungeons are shuffled together, and Mitts-locked dungeons are shuffled together.
  * Crossed door shuffle can place almost any room in any dungeon
* New option lobby_shuffle also randomizes the first room in each dungeon
* New option door_type_shuffle sets whether big key locked doors are also randomized. More options for randomizing trap doors will be added in the future
* Mirror Scroll and dungeon counters to show the number of checks/keys in each dungeon are always enabled with door shuffle
* Dungeon locations use their vanilla name, so e.g. "Ice Palace - Map Chest" might not be in Ice Palace
* Known issues:
  * Generation is slow, and on occasion is extremely slow, mostly with more advanced door shuffle. I suggest restarting generation if it is taking a long time.
  * Some settings are not allowed with door shuffle to prevent excessive generation errors
    * Standard mode + door shuffle will throw an error during generation
    * Key drop shuffle is always enabled with door shuffle, regardless of settings
    * Small key shuffle is always enabled with partitioned or crossed door shuffle, regardless of settings
  * Universal Tracker sometimes fails to connect to a doors seed
  * Universal Tracker is sometimes wrong about dungeon items, usually by believing that an item behind a locked door is accessible
  * Door shuffle is inherently a bit volatile, and is not recommended for large multiworlds

## Not Doors!
* New options for entrance shuffle to only randomize dungeon entrances
* New options to shuffle the location of Link's House and the back of Tavern in crossed entrance shuffle
* New option to enable dungeon counters that show the collected/total number of checks and keys in each dungeon
* Similar options in the YAML have been grouped together
* Hints in entrance shuffle now show the entrance(s) for their location
* Fixed a bug where Flute Shuffle didn't work correctly with Universal Tracker
* Fixed most generation errors
v1.3.0.title: v1.3.0
